Is 172.16.0.12 assigned to a host on the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN and is that host powered on / has any host-based firewall blocking ICMP?

plan.problem: Host 172.16.0.12 is not responding to ICMP from the network (25/25 pings failed).
plan.fix_summary: Collect diagnostics from router2 to determine whether the issue is on-link, ARP, interface, or upstream routing and then apply targeted remediation.

evidence: From router2: ping 172.16.0.12 -> "Sending 5... !!!!! Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 2/2/4 ms".
Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 on R2: admin_state = up, oper_state = up (phys_address ac:3a:67:20:33:70).
R2 running-config shows GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address 172.16.0.1/24 (LAN) and OSPF config includes network 172.16.0.0/24.
R2 OSPF output shows GigabitEthernet0/0/0 in area 0 with interface_state = DR (interface is up in routing).
remaining_issues: Determine why the earlier 25/25 ping failures were observed (source of those pings was not recorded).
Optional: verify host-level status on 172.16.0.12 (powered on, host firewall/ICMP settings) and retrieve ARP entry on R2 for 172.16.0.12 if you want full confirmation of layer-2 mapping.

messages.content:
What was observed
- 25 ICMP pings to 172.16.0.12 previously failed (25/25); source of those pings was not recorded.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Network path from the LAN router (R2) to 172.16.0.12 is functional — initial failure appears to be transient or host-side (host powered off/firewall) rather than a persistent routing/interface issue.

If changes were made
- No configuration changes. I ran diagnostics from R2 and collected interface/config state.

Verification
- Passed: From R2, ping 172.16.0.12 succeeded 5/5 (RTT ~2 ms). R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 is admin up/oper up. R2 config shows 172.16.0.1/24 on that interface and OSPF advertises 172.16.0.0/24.
- Remaining: Confirm why the earlier 25/25 failures were observed — identify the source of those pings and check host 172.16.0.12 itself (power, OS firewall/ICMP settings, and ARP entry on R2) if you want full closure.
